meta_title: For Founders: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Finish Your SaaS Project Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS build. Use this advanced API integration checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP in record time.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is integration friction. By decoupling your business logic from API complexity using standardized patterns and AI-driven code generation, you eliminate the technical debt that causes stalls. Focus on atomic, verifiable API endpoints to ensure your MVP moves from prototype to production reliably.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with 'almost-finished' MVPs. You start with high-octane energy, but the moment you hit the wall of complex API orchestration, your velocity drops to zero. That initial spark of innovation gets smothered by the suffocating weight of authentication flows, rate-limiting, and error handling.

But the best builders don't write boilerplate; they architect systems that write it for them. By leveraging the synthesis of Antigravity's structural scaffolding and Claude Code’s iterative execution, you can bypass the 'middle-project slump.' This guide isn't about writing code; it's about engineering a pipeline that turns your vision into a deployed, revenue-generating asset.

steps.title: Define Atomic API Contracts
steps.description: Before writing logic, define your request/response schemas. This prevents 'scope creep' and ensures your API integrations remain modular and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Generate a strictly typed OpenAPI 3.0 specification for a SaaS user-subscription management module, including error handling for 429 and 503 status codes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate typescript interfaces from the provided OpenAPI spec'

steps.title: Abstract Authentication Layers
steps.description: Don't hardcode API keys. Create a centralized service layer that handles token rotation and header injection, ensuring your core logic remains clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication interceptor pattern for Axios/Fetch that handles OAuth2 token refresh and global error logging for a Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'implement AuthInterceptor.ts to handle global headers and token expiration logic'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Rate-Limiting
steps.description: Avoid project failure due to API outages. Build in exponential backoff and circuit breakers to handle downstream failures gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust middleware function using p-retry for exponential backoff when calling third-party payment APIs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate p-retry into the payment-service.ts file for all outbound requests'

steps.title: Automate Type-Safe Integration Tests
steps.description: If it isn't tested, it's broken. Use AI to generate mocks for your external APIs so you can build and test offline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the API integration layer using MSW (Mock Service Worker) to simulate successful and failed API responses.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'generate msw handlers and unit tests for the subscription service'

steps.title: Deploy to Production with Feature Flags
steps.description: Don't release everything at once. Use feature flags to roll out your new integrations to a subset of users to minimize risk.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature flag wrapper component that checks for an environment variable to toggle between live API calls and mock data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'wrap the new API integration in a feature flag component based on process.env.ENABLE_NEW_API'

pro_tips: Always use Zod for runtime schema validation on API responses; trust nothing coming from an external source.
Log every failed request with full context to a service like Sentry; you cannot fix what you cannot measure.
Keep API logic in separate 'Gateway' files to prevent business logic from bleeding into transport logic.
Use 'Contract Testing' to ensure your API provider hasn't changed their schema unexpectedly.
When stuck, force yourself to write the documentation for the function before writing the code; the implementation usually follows immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your integration layer is robust, focus on building your frontend UI components to consume these services. Avoid adding new features until the current flow is fully deployed and verified in production.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects stall your career. Leverage the power of AI to ship faster, iterate smarter, and finally launch that SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
